reasons: Ruby and Tigreal can chain pulls and stuns to keep enemies controlled for a long teamfight window.
Ruby can follow Tigreal's engage or start a smaller pull that Tigreal expands into a bigger teamfight.

proof.summary: Tigreal and Ruby layer displacement and stun effects to keep enemies grouped and controlled.
worksBestWhen: They do not overlap all control at the exact same moment.
Follow-up damage is ready during the chained control window.
failureCases: Enemy cleanse or disengage breaks the chain before damage follow-up arrives.
Both use control at the same time and waste part of the chain.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies first, then Ruby can extend the control with her pull and stun.
Ruby can also pull targets into a better area for Atlas to finish the engage.

proof.summary: Atlas and Ruby combine repeated displacement tools to keep enemies inside the team's damage area.
worksBestWhen: Ruby times her pull after Atlas's drag instead of before it.
The team has damage ready when the enemies are grouped.
failureCases: Both use control at the same time and waste part of the chain.
Ruby pulls enemies away from the team's damage zone.

reasons: Angela can reinforce Ruby's frontline sustain when Ruby commits to a pull-heavy fight.
Ruby benefits because she often stays inside enemy range to control targets.

proof.summary: Angela helps Ruby survive while Ruby keeps enemies controlled in close range.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches as Ruby commits into the fight.
Ruby has follow-up damage nearby for her control.
failureCases: Ruby is burst down before the shield lands.
Ruby gets isolated away from follow-up.

reasons: Ruby can pull enemies into Gord's area damage and beam path.
Gord benefits because Ruby's control helps prevent enemies from walking out immediately.

proof.summary: Ruby controls and groups targets, while Gord follows with area magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Gord is positioned safely before Ruby pulls enemies.
Ruby pulls enemies into Gord's skill path.
failureCases: Ruby pulls enemies away from Gord's cast angle.
Gord is dived before the follow-up.

reasons: Ruby can pull enemies into Kagura's umbrella combo area.
Kagura adds burst and additional control after Ruby starts the fight.

proof.summary: Ruby starts the close-range control, while Kagura adds umbrella burst and follow-up control from safer range.
worksBestWhen: Kagura places umbrella before Ruby's pull or tracks target movement quickly.
Ruby pulls targets into Kagura's combo area.
failureCases: Ruby pulls enemies away from Kagura's umbrella position.
Kagura commits before Ruby confirms the pull.
